title: Bound
description: A sound bound on an approximate operation, with the BASIS by which it was obtained. Projection of RFC-0001 §4.3 (Bound, BoundBasis) reconciled with ADR-010 (the {eps, delta, strength} certificate vocabulary) and RFC-0002 §3 (a certificate carries Bound + BoundBasis).

x-mycelium.$comment: RECONCILIATION (OQ-3, raised to corpus owner): RFC-0001 §4.3's grammar attaches `basis` only to CapacityBound, but invariants M-I2/M-I3/M-I4 read `bound.basis` for ANY bound, and RFC-0002 §3 pairs every certificate Bound with a BoundBasis. This schema makes `basis` a required companion of every Bound kind, which is the only reading consistent with M-I2/3/4. A one-line RFC-0001 §4.3 clarification is proposed to fold `basis` out to the common Bound level.

BoundBasis.description: How the bound was obtained. Determines the honest GuaranteeStrength: Proven only with a checked-instantiation cited theorem; else Empirical (trials) or UserDeclared (asserted, always flagged).

oneOf.description: e.g. 'Clarkson-Ubaru-Yang 2023'. Side-conditions (dimension, sparsity class, model) MUST be checked where this basis is asserted (RFC-0002 §3; RFC-0003 §5).
User-asserted, not yet validated. Any tool presenting the value MUST surface a 'declared, unverified' marker (M-I4; VR-5).

NormKind.$comment: RFC-0001 §4.3 names `norm: NormKind` for ErrorBound but does not enumerate it normatively (open registry; OQ-4). Kept as a non-empty string until the registry is fixed; common intended members: L1, L2, Linf, Rel.

oneOf.description: epsilon-magnitude bound; composes via ADR-010's affine-arithmetic ErrorBound kernel.
failure-probability bound; composes via ADR-010's union-bound ProbBound kernel.
expected crosstalk with optional tail; VSA content per RFC-0003 / T0.2.
VSA superposition capacity (items into dim). The MAP-I/sparse Proven cases cite T0.2 theorems via basis.ProvenThm (RFC-0003 §4); the M-001 LH probe confirms the checked-instantiation strategy.
